description: God class handling I/O, parsing, buffer management, number conversion, and error reporting.
reasoning: The class exceeds 4,000 lines and encapsulates multiple distinct responsibilities: stream-based I/O handling, low-level JSON token parsing, buffer management, complex numeric parsing/validation, and exception reporting. These responsibilities should be decomposed into separate components (e.g., an InputReader, a Tokenizer, and a ValueParser).

description: Fat class mixing business logic (generating JSON) with low-level stream/buffer management and character escaping.
reasoning: The class handles high-level JSON writing API, low-level binary output buffering, complex character encoding/escaping logic, and pretty-printing. This violates SRP as it changes for reasons related to both the JSON format definition and the underlying byte-stream output mechanisms.

description: Fat interface/abstract class forcing implementations to handle too many unrelated writing concerns.
reasoning: The class forces all implementations (JSON, binary, etc.) to implement methods for object ID writing, type ID writing, array/object manipulation, raw data writing, and POJO writing, despite many implementations only needing a subset of these capabilities (e.g., raw stream generators shouldn't necessarily implement high-level type ID logic).
